content:
# Joshua Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 227–228; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), LXX | **Source Images:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_003_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_003_page_002.jpg`; `../../source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/JOS_003_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `JOS_003_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## Israel Prepares to Cross the Yarden (vv. 1–6)

**1** And Yehoshua rose early in the morning, and they set out from Shittim, and they came unto the Yarden — he and all the sons of Yisra'el — and they lodged there before they crossed over.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּשְׁכֵּם יְהוֹשֻׁעַ בַּבֹּקֶר וַיִּסְעוּ מֵהַשִּׁטִּים וַיָּבֹאוּ עַד־הַיַּרְדֵּן הוּא וְכׇל־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל וַיָּלִנוּ שָׁם טֶרֶם יַעֲבֹרוּ. The verb וַיַּשְׁכֵּם (vayashkem) preserves the characteristic "rose early" idiom. The Shittim (הַשִּׁטִּים, "the acacias") is the last encampment east of the Yarden, identified with Abel-shittim of Numbers 33:49. LXX (Vaticanus): καὶ ὤρθρισεν Ἰησοῦς τὸ πρωΐ — "and Iesous rose early in the morning."

**2** And it was at the end of three days, and the officers passed through the midst of the camp.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְהִי מִקְצֵה שְׁלֹשֶׁת יָמִים וַיַּעַבְרוּ הַשֹּׁטְרִים בְּקֶרֶב הַמַּחֲנֶה. The שֹׁטְרִים (shoterim, "officers/scribes") are the administrative officials who relay commands, distinct from military commanders. LXX: καὶ ἐγένετο μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας — uses μετά ("after") rather than a literal rendering of מִקְצֵה ("from the end of").

**3** And they commanded the people, saying, "When you see the ark of the covenant of YHWH your God, and the priests, the Levites, carrying it, then you shall set out from your place and go after it.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְצַוּוּ אֶת־הָעָם לֵאמֹר כִּרְאוֹתְכֶם אֵת אֲרוֹן בְּרִית־יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵיכֶם וְהַכֹּהֲנִים הַלְוִיִּם נֹשְׂאִים אֹתוֹ וְאַתֶּם תִּסְעוּ מִמְּקוֹמְכֶם וַהֲלַכְתֶּם אַחֲרָיו. The phrase הַכֹּהֲנִים הַלְוִיִּם ("the priests, the Levites") is a characteristic Deuteronomistic expression identifying the priestly tribe. The waw-consecutive וַהֲלַכְתֶּם continues the command chain. LXX: τὴν κιβωτὸν τῆς διαθήκης κυρίου τοῦ θεοῦ ὑμῶν — renders בְּרִית as διαθήκη ("covenant/testament").

**4** However, [let there be] a distance between you and between it — about two thousand cubits by measure. You shall not draw near to it — so that you may know the way which you shall go in it, for you have not passed [over] in the way from yesterday [and] the third [day before]."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אַךְ רָחוֹק יִהְיֶה בֵּינֵיכֶם וּבֵינָיו כְּאַלְפַּיִם אַמָּה בַּמִּדָּה אַל־תִּקְרְבוּ אֵלָיו לְמַעַן אֲשֶׁר תֵּדְעוּ אֶת־הַדֶּרֶךְ אֲשֶׁר תֵּלְכוּ־בָהּ כִּי לֹא עֲבַרְתֶּם בַּדֶּרֶךְ מִתְּמוֹל שִׁלְשׁוֹם. The expression מִתְּמוֹל שִׁלְשׁוֹם ("from yesterday, the third [day]") is the Hebrew idiom for "previously/before now." The two-thousand-cubit distance (approximately one thousand meters) ensures the ark remains visible as a guide while maintaining sacral distance. LXX: ὅσον δισχιλίας πήχεις — "about two thousand cubits."

**5** And Yehoshua said to the people, "Sanctify yourselves, for tomorrow YHWH will do wonders in your midst."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוֹשֻׁעַ אֶל־הָעָם הִתְקַדָּשׁוּ כִּי מָחָר יַעֲשֶׂה יְהוָה בְּקִרְבְּכֶם נִפְלָאוֹת. The hitpa'el הִתְקַדָּשׁוּ ("sanctify yourselves") implies ritual purification — washing garments, abstaining from contact with impurity — echoing the preparation at Sinai (Exodus 19:10–11). The term נִפְלָאוֹת ("wonders") links this crossing to the exodus traditions. LXX: ἁγνίσασθε εἰς αὔριον, ὅτι αὔριον ποιήσει ἐν ὑμῖν κύριος θαυμαστά — "purify yourselves for tomorrow."

**6** And Yehoshua spoke to the priests, saying, "Take up the ark of the covenant and cross over before the people." And they took up the ark of the covenant and went before the people.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוֹשֻׁעַ אֶל־הַכֹּהֲנִים לֵאמֹר שְׂאוּ אֶת־אֲרוֹן הַבְּרִית וְעִבְרוּ לִפְנֵי הָעָם וַיִּשְׂאוּ אֶת־אֲרוֹן הַבְּרִית וַיֵּלְכוּ לִפְנֵי הָעָם. The command and its immediate execution form a characteristic narrative pattern in Joshua: command given, command obeyed. LXX: ἄρατε τὴν κιβωτὸν τῆς διαθήκης — matches the Hebrew precisely.

---

## YHWH Speaks to Yehoshua — The Living God Among You (vv. 7–13)

**7** And YHWH said to Yehoshua, "This day I will begin to make you great in the eyes of all Yisra'el, so that they will know that just as I was with Mosheh, I will be with you.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוָה אֶל־יְהוֹשֻׁעַ הַיּוֹם הַזֶּה אָחֵל גַּדֶּלְךָ בְּעֵינֵי כׇל־יִשְׂרָאֵל אֲשֶׁר יֵדְעוּן כִּי כַּאֲשֶׁר הָיִיתִי עִם־מֹשֶׁה אֶהְיֶה עִמָּךְ. The verb אָחֵל ("I will begin") uses the hif'il of חלל. The divine promise אֶהְיֶה עִמָּךְ ("I will be with you") echoes the commissioning formula of 1:5. The plural יֵדְעוּן (with paragogic nun) is a distinctive form. LXX: ἐν τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ταύτῃ ἄρχομαι ὑψῶσαί σε — "I begin to exalt you."

**8** And you shall command the priests carrying the ark of the covenant, saying, 'When you come unto the edge of the waters of the Yarden, in the Yarden you shall stand.'"

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְאַתָּה תְּצַוֶּה אֶת־הַכֹּהֲנִים נֹשְׂאֵי אֲרוֹן־הַבְּרִית לֵאמֹר כְּבֹאֲכֶם עַד־קְצֵה מֵי הַיַּרְדֵּן בַּיַּרְדֵּן תַּעֲמֹדוּ. The prepositional phrase בַּיַּרְדֵּן תַּעֲמֹדוּ ("in the Yarden you shall stand") places the verb at the end for emphasis — they are to stand still within the river itself. LXX: ἐν τῷ Ἰορδάνῃ στήσεσθε — "in the Iordanes you shall stand."

**9** And Yehoshua said to the sons of Yisra'el, "Draw near here and hear the words of YHWH your God."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוֹשֻׁעַ אֶל־בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל גֹּשׁוּ הֵנָּה וְשִׁמְעוּ אֶת־דִּבְרֵי יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵיכֶם. The imperative גֹּשׁוּ ("draw near") summons the people for a solemn declaration. LXX: προσαγάγετε ὧδε — "come forward here."

**10** And Yehoshua said, "By this you shall know that a living God [is] in your midst, and [that] he will certainly dispossess [hif'il infinitive absolute + yiqtol: drive out, drive out] from before you the Kena'ani [Canaanite] and the Chitti [Hittite] and the Chivvi [Hivite] and the Perizzi [Perizzite] and the Girgashi [Girgashite] and the Emori [Amorite] and the Yevusi [Jebusite].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיֹּאמֶר יְהוֹשֻׁעַ בְּזֹאת תֵּדְעוּן כִּי אֵל חַי בְּקִרְבְּכֶם וְהוֹרֵשׁ יוֹרִישׁ מִפְּנֵיכֶם אֶת־הַכְּנַעֲנִי וְאֶת־הַחִתִּי וְאֶת־הַחִוִּי וְאֶת־הַפְּרִזִּי וְאֶת־הַגִּרְגָּשִׁי וְהָאֱמֹרִי וְהַיְבוּסִי. The construction וְהוֹרֵשׁ יוֹרִישׁ is an infinitive absolute (הוֹרֵשׁ) + yiqtol (יוֹרִישׁ) expressing certainty — "he will certainly dispossess." The seven-nation list is a standard enumeration of the peoples of the land (cf. Deuteronomy 7:1). The phrase אֵל חַי ("a living God") contrasts with the dead idols of the nations. LXX: ἐν τούτῳ γνώσεσθε ὅτι θεὸς ζῶν ἐν ὑμῖν — "by this you shall know that a living God [is] among you."

**11** Behold, the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth [is] crossing over before you into the Yarden.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הִנֵּה אֲרוֹן הַבְּרִית אֲדוֹן כׇּל־הָאָרֶץ עֹבֵר לִפְנֵיכֶם בַּיַּרְדֵּן. The title אֲדוֹן כׇּל־הָאָרֶץ ("Lord of all the earth") is rare and theologically significant — it appears also in Micah 4:13, Zechariah 4:14, 6:5, and Psalm 97:5. EMET preserves "Lord" (אֲדוֹן) here as a distinct title from YHWH, as the Hebrew uses אֲדוֹן not the Tetragrammaton. LXX: ἡ κιβωτὸς διαθήκης κυρίου πάσης τῆς γῆς — "the ark of the covenant of the Lord of all the earth."

**12** And now, take for yourselves twelve men from the tribes of Yisra'el, one man, one man for [each] tribe.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְעַתָּה קְחוּ לָכֶם שְׁנֵים עָשָׂר אִישׁ מִשִּׁבְטֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל אִישׁ־אֶחָד אִישׁ־אֶחָד לַשָּׁבֶט. The distributive construction אִישׁ־אֶחָד אִישׁ־אֶחָד ("one man, one man") is a Hebrew idiom for "one each." This command anticipates the memorial stones of chapter 4. LXX: δώδεκα ἄνδρας — "twelve men."

**13** And it shall be, when the soles of the feet of the priests carrying the ark of YHWH, Lord of all the earth, rest in the waters of the Yarden, the waters of the Yarden shall be cut off — the waters descending from above — and they shall stand [as] one heap."

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהָיָה כְּנוֹחַ כַּפּוֹת רַגְלֵי הַכֹּהֲנִים נֹשְׂאֵי אֲרוֹן יְהוָה אֲדוֹן כׇּל־הָאָרֶץ בְּמֵי הַיַּרְדֵּן מֵי הַיַּרְדֵּן יִכָּרֵתוּן הַמַּיִם הַיֹּרְדִים מִלְמָעְלָה וְיַעַמְדוּ נֵד אֶחָד. The verb יִכָּרֵתוּן (nif'al of כרת with paragogic nun) means "shall be cut off." The noun נֵד ("heap") echoes Exodus 15:8 where the waters of the Reed Sea stood as a נֵד — a deliberate literary parallel linking the Jordan crossing to the exodus event. LXX: τὸ ὕδωρ τοῦ Ἰορδάνου ἐκλείψει — "the water of the Iordanes shall fail/cease."

---

## The Crossing — The Waters Stand Still (vv. 14–17)

**14** And it was, when the people set out from their tents to cross over the Yarden, and the priests carrying the ark of the covenant [were] before the people,

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיְהִי בִּנְסֹעַ הָעָם מֵאׇהֳלֵיהֶם לַעֲבֹר אֶת־הַיַּרְדֵּן וְהַכֹּהֲנִים נֹשְׂאֵי הָאָרוֹן הַבְּרִית לִפְנֵי הָעָם. The infinitive construct בִּנְסֹעַ ("when setting out") marks the temporal clause. LXX: ὡς δὲ ἐξῆρεν ὁ λαὸς ἐκ τῶν σκηνωμάτων αὐτῶν — "and when the people departed from their tents."

**15** and when those carrying the ark came unto the Yarden, and the feet of the priests carrying the ark were dipped in the edge of the waters — and the Yarden is full over all its banks all the days of harvest —

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּכְבוֹא נֹשְׂאֵי הָאָרוֹן עַד־הַיַּרְדֵּן וְרַגְלֵי הַכֹּהֲנִים נֹשְׂאֵי הָאָרוֹן נִטְבְּלוּ בִּקְצֵה הַמָּיִם וְהַיַּרְדֵּן מָלֵא עַל־כׇּל־גְּדוֹתָיו כֹּל יְמֵי קָצִיר. The verb נִטְבְּלוּ (nif'al of טבל, "were dipped/immersed") is the same root from which later "baptism" (via Greek βαπτίζω) draws its imagery. The parenthetical notice that the Yarden was in flood stage during harvest (barley harvest, March–April) heightens the miraculous nature of the event. LXX: ὁ δὲ Ἰορδάνης ἐπλήρου καθ' ὅλην τὴν κρηπῖδα αὐτοῦ — "and the Iordanes filled along its whole bank."

**16** and the waters descending from above stood [and] rose up [as] one heap, very far away, at Adam, the city which [is] beside Tsaretan, and those [waters] descending upon the sea of the Aravah, the Salt Sea, were completely cut off. And the people crossed over opposite Yericho.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַמְדוּ הַמַּיִם הַיֹּרְדִים מִלְמַעְלָה קָמוּ נֵד־אֶחָד הַרְחֵק מְאֹד בְּאָדָם [כתיב: מאדם] הָעִיר אֲשֶׁר מִצַּד צָרְתָן וְהַיֹּרְדִים עַל יָם הָעֲרָבָה יָם־הַמֶּלַח תַּמּוּ נִכְרָתוּ וְהָעָם עָבְרוּ נֶגֶד יְרִיחוֹ. The city of אָדָם ("Adam") is identified with Tell ed-Damiyeh, approximately thirty kilometers north of the crossing point. The Ketiv reads מאדם (me-Adam, "from Adam") while the Qere reads בְּאָדָם (be-Adam, "at Adam"). The double verb תַּמּוּ נִכְרָתוּ ("were finished, were cut off") emphasizes the completeness of the stoppage. The נֵד אֶחָד ("one heap") again echoes the Song of the Sea (Exodus 15:8). LXX: ἕως μέρους Καριαθιαριμ — the LXX reads a different place name, "Kariathiarim," likely a corruption or alternative tradition for צָרְתָן.

**17** And the priests carrying the ark of the covenant of YHWH stood firm on dry ground in the midst of the Yarden, and all Yisra'el [were] crossing over on dry ground, until all the nation finished crossing over the Yarden.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַיַּעַמְדוּ הַכֹּהֲנִים נֹשְׂאֵי הָאָרוֹן בְּרִית־יְהוָה בֶּחָרָבָה בְּתוֹךְ הַיַּרְדֵּן הָכֵן וְכׇל־יִשְׂרָאֵל עֹבְרִים בֶּחָרָבָה עַד אֲשֶׁר־תַּמּוּ כׇּל־הַגּוֹי לַעֲבֹר אֶת־הַיַּרְדֵּן. The adverb הָכֵן means "firmly, securely" — the priests stood immovable. The noun חָרָבָה ("dry ground") echoes the exodus crossing of the Reed Sea (Exodus 14:21). The shift from יִשְׂרָאֵל ("Israel") to הַגּוֹי ("the nation") in the final clause is deliberate — the tribal confederation is here named as a single national entity completing its passage into the promised land. LXX: ἐπὶ ξηρᾶς ἐν μέσῳ τοῦ Ἰορδάνου — "upon dry [ground] in the midst of the Iordanes." The term הָכֵן is rendered ἑτοίμως ("readily") in some LXX traditions.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

### Key Hebrew Terms Preserved
- **YHWH** (יְהוָה): The divine name, preserved as written in the Leningrad Codex, not replaced with "the LORD."
- **Yehoshua** (יְהוֹשֻׁעַ): "YHWH saves" — the Hebrew name preserved rather than the anglicized "Joshua."
- **Yarden** (יַרְדֵּן): "The descender" — from the root ירד (yarad, "to go down"), preserving the etymological meaning.
- **Adon kol-ha'arets** (אֲדוֹן כׇּל־הָאָרֶץ): "Lord of all the earth" — a distinct title from YHWH, using אֲדוֹן (sovereign lord), rendered as "Lord" (not "LORD").
- **Ned echad** (נֵד אֶחָד): "One heap" — the same word used for the waters at the Reed Sea (Exodus 15:8), creating a deliberate intertextual echo.
- **Charavah** (חָרָבָה): "Dry ground" — also echoing the Reed Sea crossing (Exodus 14:21), linking the two events.

### Infinitive Absolute Construction
- Verse 10: וְהוֹרֵשׁ יוֹרִישׁ — The infinitive absolute הוֹרֵשׁ followed by the finite verb יוֹרִישׁ expresses certainty and emphasis: "he will certainly dispossess" (literally: "dispossessing, he will dispossess").

### Ketiv/Qere Variants
- Verse 16: The Ketiv (written text) reads מֵאָדָם (me-Adam, "from Adam") while the Qere (reading tradition) reads בְּאָדָם (be-Adam, "at/in Adam"). EMET follows the Qere tradition here, as is standard practice.

### Paragogic Nun Forms
- Verse 7: יֵדְעוּן ("they shall know") — the paragogic nun (ן-) is an archaic or emphatic verbal ending, sometimes associated with northern dialectal features or elevated discourse.
- Verse 13: יִכָּרֵתוּן ("they shall be cut off") — same paragogic nun form, lending solemnity to the prophetic declaration.

### LXX Divergences from the MT
- Verse 2: LXX uses μετὰ τρεῖς ἡμέρας ("after three days") for MT's מִקְצֵה שְׁלֹשֶׁת יָמִים ("at the end of three days") — semantically equivalent but structurally different.
- Verse 13: LXX renders יִכָּרֵתוּן with ἐκλείψει ("shall fail/cease") rather than a literal "shall be cut off," softening the violent imagery of the Hebrew כרת root.
- Verse 16: LXX reads Καριαθιαριμ where MT has צָרְתָן (Tsaretan) — a significant geographical variant suggesting either a textual corruption in the Greek transmission or an alternative local tradition.
- Verse 17: LXX does not clearly render הָכֵן ("firmly"); some traditions read ἑτοίμως ("readily/preparedly"), which captures a different nuance.

### Theological-Narrative Observations
1. **Exodus Typology:** The chapter is saturated with exodus echoes — נֵד ("heap"), חָרָבָה ("dry ground"), the parting of waters, the command to sanctify — deliberately presenting the Jordan crossing as a second exodus.
2. **Seven Nations:** The list of seven peoples in verse 10 signals the completeness of YHWH's dispossession — seven being the number of fullness.
3. **The Ark as Vanguard:** The ark of the covenant goes before the people (vv. 3, 6, 11, 14) — YHWH himself leads the entry into the land.
4. **Harvest Flood:** The notice in verse 15 that the Yarden was at flood stage during harvest is not parenthetical filler but theological emphasis — the miracle occurs at the river's maximum, not at a shallow ford.
